channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft get more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: I repurposed Karpathy's LLM Wiki for product discovery. It worked surprisingly well.
item.description: I was playing with Karpathy's LLM Wiki and realized it could be re-applied to my manual workflow as a PM. Normally I identify quotes from transcripts, create user stories, group them into...

item.title: RSC as a serializer, not a model
item.description: The most interesting thing about how TanStack Start integrates React Server Components is not what it does with them. It is the shape of the API. You do not write a Server Component. You do not...

item.title: Express is the only major framework where FastAPI-style automated API docs still don't exist
item.description: I am a backend developer. Every time I start an Express project I need API docs (like swagger of fastAPI but in express). I look up the options and every single one has a dealbreaker....

item.title: I got tired of AI agents trashing my codebase, so I built a skill to fix that
item.description: Every AI coding agent I've used hits the same wall. It always starts the same way. You ask it to add a feature. It rewrites half the file in its preferred naming convention. Your snake_case Python...

item.title: 3 LINQ Mistakes That Hurt Backend API Performance in .NET
item.description: LINQ looks clean and harmless in C#. But in real backend APIs, small mistakes can quietly turn into serious performance issues. Here are 3 I’ve seen (and made) while working with .NET and Entity...

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Apple wants $199 for a Touch ID keyboard. I shipped a free one in Swift!
item.description: I have a Mac mini. Apple's official path to fingerprint authentication on a Mac mini is buying their $199 Magic Keyboard with Touch ID. The new MacBook Neo charges $100 extra for the variant with...

item.title: What I Look for When Hiring Remote Full-Stack Developers (React + Node.js)
item.description: What I Look for When Hiring Remote Full-Stack Developers (React + Node.js) After years of building and leading remote engineering teams, I've reviewed hundreds of applications, conducted dozens of...

item.title: I Asked AI to Audit My Last 3 Failed Projects — Here's What It Found
item.description: Two years ago, I was managing three concurrent projects. All three ran over budget. Two missed their deadlines. One got cancelled. I blamed external factors at the time: changing requirements,...

item.title: Conspiracy theories are swirling about the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: On Saturday evening, a room full of journalists, media personalities, and senior members of Donald Trump's administration descended into chaos as gunshots rang out at the White House...

item.title: I Built CrabPDF: a Privacy-First PDF Editor That Runs Locally in the Browser
item.description: PDF tools are everywhere, yes. This is what I believed. But every time I needed to edit quickly a PDF from another computer, I found always the same problems: upload your file, create account,...

item.title: Guardrails in AI: Keeping LLMs Safe
item.description: 🤔 Imagine asking an AI agent to generate a database query… and it returns something wrong — or worse, unsafe. The problem isn’t just intelligence. It’s control. That’s where guardrails come in. ⚡...

item.title: Why the Steam Controller is (and isn’t) a big deal
item.description: Most PC gamers already have a controller they love using with Steam - a Sony DualSense, a 8BitDo Ultimate, a Nintendo Switch Pro, or something else. Part of that love comes from Steam treating...

item.title: Valve’s new Steam Controller isn’t perfect, but I’m buying one anyway
item.description: After my brief hands-on last year with Valve's new Steam Controller, I said it might be my dream controller. I've been looking for a controller with the customization and sheer function available...

item.title: Valve launches the Steam Controller without the Steam Machine
item.description: Last November, Valve introduced the world to its new vision of living room gaming: the Steam Machine and Steam Controller. Then, RAMageddon. Memory shortages forced Valve to delay all its hardware...

item.title: We reviewed Valve’s new Steam Controller, ask us anything
item.description: Hey hey, it's Jay Peters, senior reporter at The Verge. Today, Valve finally announced that the second version of the Steam Controller - and the first piece of Valve's slate of new gaming hardware...

item.title: Samsung’s first smart glasses have leaked
item.description: The upcoming Samsung Galaxy Glasses look nearly identical to Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses in leaked images from Android Headlines. The glasses, which could be announced at Google I/O next month,...

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI’s famed AGI agreement is dead
item.description: OpenAI and Microsoft's partnership-turned-situationship just got even less committed. And a clause about artificial general intelligence, which has for years dictated the future of their deal, has...

item.title: The missing step between hype and profit
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In February, I picked up a flyer at an anti-AI march in...

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

item.title: The Philips Hue Sync Box 8K is 30 percent off
item.description: The Philips Hue Play HDMI Sync Box 8K is a great gadget to buy if you want to put your Hue lights to good use while watching a movie or playing video games. It syncs with your lights to mirror the...

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are set to face off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of tech’s leading AI startup, OpenAI. The trial begins with jury selection on April 27th, as Musk...

item.title: The Verge’s 2026 Mother’s Day gift guide
item.description: Whether it's managing a busy home or looking out for everyone around them, moms spend a lot of time every day caring for others. Mother's Day, May 10th, is an opportunity to return the favor, so...

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web....

item.title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
item.description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed...

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.description: The phone could go into mass production in 2028, an analyst says.

item.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. While consumer-facing...
